entries.statement: Develop and maintain the enterprise data and AI strategy, aligned to business objectives, with defined investment priorities, milestones, accountability assignments, and success metrics.
Design enterprise information architecture and systems to support organizational objectives, including alignment of data architecture with business strategy and capability delivery.
Establish and operate the enterprise data governance program, including council structure, policy library, stewardship network, data domain ownership registry, and governance metrics.
Develop and enforce enterprise data management standards, requirements, and specifications across all data systems and operations.
Conduct enterprise data maturity assessments against recognized frameworks and define improvement roadmaps with measurable milestones and accountable owners.
Establish and maintain the enterprise data catalog, including asset classification, metadata management, data lineage documentation, and discoverability infrastructure.

Establish and operate comprehensive AI risk assessment, monitoring, and compliance infrastructure, including risk evaluation frameworks, performance thresholds, drift detection, incident reporting, and regulatory adherence.
Operate the Data Governance Council, including scheduling, agenda preparation, issue tracking, decision logging, and follow-through on action items.
Manage the enterprise data stewardship network, including steward onboarding, role clarity, tools access, accountability assignment, and regular steward community engagement.
Produce and distribute regular data governance program status reports, including maturity progress, open issues, policy violations, compliance status, and upcoming regulatory obligations.
Maintain the business glossary definitions, data asset catalog entries, and data lineage documentation for the assigned data domain.
Participate in the data governance council and stewardship community, representing the assigned domain's interests and escalating unresolved issues through established channels.
Manage data access control, including reviewing and approving access requests, data sharing agreements, use authorizations, and ensuring compliance with established policies.
Conduct data and AI strategy engagements, including current state assessment, future state design, gap analysis, and roadmap development with defined milestones and investment estimates.
Process data access requests per defined procedures, including request validation, approval routing, provisioning, access log documentation, and ongoing access review.
Assess data quality, completeness, consistency, and fitness for purpose across enterprise data assets, documenting quality impacts on analytical conclusions and decision support.
Establish and operate data collection, curation, and acquisition governance processes, ensuring data quality, provenance tracking, and compliance with requirements.
Govern data mining and data warehousing programs, including establishing procedures, validating infrastructure, and ensuring analytical processes meet compliance and quality standards.
Define and manage organizational metrics and KPIs for data governance and AI outcomes, partnering with business areas to establish consistent measurement frameworks.
Lead selection, contracting, and performance oversight of strategic data and AI technology vendors and platform providers, managing third-party risks and integration.
Communicate data and AI governance status, outcomes, and value to executive leadership and decision makers, demonstrating how initiatives address organizational challenges.
Establish responsible AI practices and ethical frameworks, including defining organizational AI risk tolerance, ethical guidelines, and mitigation strategies aligned with governance policy.
Analyze and define enterprise data requirements and specifications, translating business needs into technical specifications and ensuring alignment with strategic objectives.
